name: Consolidate duplicated mobile lib helpers and micro-modules
description: apps/mobile/src/lib/ contains several micro-modules (1-7 lines) that export a single type or function with no co-location rationale, causing needless import churn. Two clear consolidation targets: learning-subject-tints.ts (4 lines — single interface LearningSubjectTint) and copy-register.ts (7 lines — CopyRegister wrapper over ActiveProfileRole). Both are used in under 5 call sites and belong in an existing home.
acceptanceCriteria:
Current state: apps/mobile/src/lib/learning-subject-tints.ts (4 lines, interface only, used in ~4 components) and apps/mobile/src/lib/copy-register.ts (7 lines, CopyRegister type + copyRegisterFor(), used in ~3 screens) are standalone micro-modules with no coherent grouping rationale.

Target: learning-subject-tints.ts merged into apps/mobile/src/lib/subject-tints.ts (the related tint helper — grep: `import.*learning-subject-tints` to find all callers, update to import from subject-tints); copy-register.ts inlined into its 2-3 callers or folded into apps/mobile/src/lib/profile.ts alongside the existing profile helpers.

Approach: (1) Run `grep -r 'learning-subject-tints\|LearningSubjectTint' apps/mobile/src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"` — update all import sites. (2) Run `grep -r 'copy-register\|CopyRegister\|copyRegisterFor' apps/mobile/src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"` — inline or re-home. (3) Delete the emptied files.

Scope boundary: apps/mobile/src/lib/ only — do not touch date-formatting trio (format-relative-date.ts, format-datetime.ts, local-date.ts) which are intentionally split by ICU/TZ concerns.

Verification: pnpm exec nx lint mobile passes; pnpm exec nx run mobile:typecheck passes; jest --findRelatedTests on changed import sites green. No new files created — consolidation only.

Forward guard: no dedicated lint rule needed; the GC1 ratchet already prevents new jest.mock violations; file-size here is a judgement concern, not a CI gate.
